msg: assignment discards 'const' qualifier from pointer target type (CType(CPointer(CUnsigned(CLong())),true,false,false) = CType(CPointer(CUnsigned(CLong())),false,false,true))
reasoning: Duplicate of FAMILY_45 (same file, same lines 133-137, same presence condition def(CONFIG_NUMA)&def(CONFIG_PCI)&!def(const), same message). As with FAMILY_45, the '!def(const)' term is an artifact of the tool treating the C keyword 'const' as a preprocessor configuration macro. No real Linux kernel build satisfies !def(const). The const-qualifier discarding warning is entirely fabricated by the tool's type-lifting mechanism. False positive.
